title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · Mastery Check
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
Compare a DBMS with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs.
Trace a query through parser, planner, executor, storage, buffer, transaction, and recovery responsibilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Data Lifecycle · changed case · Quiz Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz support system has 4 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

Which action demonstrates Data Lifecycle without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Data Lifecycle · changed case · Quiz Data Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction. For Data Lifecycle · changed case · Quiz Data Lifecycle, keep Data Lifecycle connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Data Lifecycle result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM/IEEE-CS/AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023: Data Management — Controls institutional breadth, learning outcomes, modeling, querying, processing, internals, security, privacy, analytics, and distributed-system coverage.
